RN write ceftazidime instead of ceftriaxone
Rn write loratine 10mg BID instead of loperamid 2mg BID
RN write fluconazole iv BID instead of OD.
RN write lovenox 40mg PO instead of sub.

records.Remarks/ Comments: According to the home medication reconciliation form, this patient was on advagraf to prevent allograft rejection (liver). On admission home medications were reconcilated by an ID physician. The physician did not specified the form of tacrolimus on the medical order. On the pharmacy request prograft was ordered instead of Advagraf. Prograf should be taken twice daily (1 mg po Q12h) instead of once per day (2 mg po Q24h). After contact with the attending physician the order was changed to Advagraf 2 mg Q24h.
Based on impaired renal function (GFR=15 mL/min), attending physician was advised to discontinue Lipanty (fenofibrate), which is contraindicated in cases with a GFR of < 30 mL/min.

The patient was on Atrovent nebulizer (ipratropium). New order was given to start combivent, which contains ipratropium and salbutamol, without discontinuation of atrovent. RN was contacted to recommend discontinuation of atrovent.

records.error_type: Improper dose (over, under or extra dose)
records.Remarks/ Comments: In case of MRSA infection,  Bactrim should be given in a dose of  4 mg/kg/day. ID physician was contacted to make the dose 320/1600 mg  (2 tabs) Q12h.

records.drug: Augmentin 1g PO Q12h and Meropenem 1g IV Q8h
records.Remarks/ Comments: Patient was on Augmentin to treat diabetic foot infection caused by Enterococcus Avium. Based on a new wound culture result that demonstrated Sphingomonas paucimobilis, ID physician initiated meropenem course. Both agents are beta-lactam antibiotics with overlapping spectra. Meropenem covers everything augmentin does- and more. ID physician was contacted to advise discontinuation of the weaker agent (augmentin) upon initiation of meropenem.

records.drug:
Bactrim 160/800 mg PO &
Mirtazapine 30 mg PO OD &
Moxifloxacin 400 mg PO OD
records.Remarks/ Comments: This drug combination increase QTc interval. Due to initiation of Ambisome course, which is known to have hypokalemia as a side effect, the risk of QT-interval prolongation is increased. ICU physician was contacted to recommend temporary discontinuation of mirtazapine, and close monitoring of ECG and potassium level.
Based on impaired renal function (GFR=15 mL/min), attending physician was advised to discontinue Lipanty (fenofibrate), which is contraindicated in cases with a GFR of < 30 mL/min.

According to the data in the "Clinical Laboratory Information System", the patient had E.coli-CRE in blood. However, tygacil distributes extensively into tissues, resulting in low blood levels. Hence, the ID physician was contacted to suggest other antimicrobial agent which is indicated for the treatment of bacteremia.

records.error_type: Omission error(No drug for medical condition)
records.Remarks/ Comments: According to the guidelines the patient requires SUP (elderly patient in ICU ward, on lovenox, aspirin and solumedrol). ICU  resident physician was advised to add PPI.

records.error_type: Improper dose (over, under or extra dose)
records.Remarks/ Comments: The standard neonatal maintanence dose of fluconazole should be 3-6 mg/kg/day. The patient received a subtherapeutic dose.  Attending physician was advised to increase the dose to a minimum dose of  4.8 mg IV OD (3 mg/kg; body weight:1.6 kg).

records.error_type: Improper dose (over, under or extra dose)
records.Remarks/ Comments: In cases of septic shock, hydrocortisone should be given in a dose of 50 mg IV Q6h or 100 mg IV Q8h. Resident physician was advised to increase the dose or to discontinue.

records.error_type: Improper dose (over, under or extra dose)
records.ACTION TAKEN: Send pharmacists note to physician for clarification
records.Remarks/ Comments: Inappropriate dose of imipenem. According to the guidelines the dose should be 1g IV Q8h or 0.5g IV Q6h. ID physician gave an approval for 0.5g IF Q6h.The RN was contacted to suggest dose correction.

Patient is diagnosed with Tuberous Sclerosis Complex (cardiac myopathy). Since tacrolimus (Prograft) is not indicated for the treatment of Tuberous Sclerosis Complex and because prograft should be given twice a day (instead of OD), the prescriber was contacted to check the order.  Everolimus should be given instead of tacrolimus. The physician was not familiar with the brand names of tacrolimus.

records.error_type: Omission error(No drug for medical condition)
records.Remarks/ Comments: Based on patients' medical history (DM, HTN, HF, CKD), clinical pharmacist recommended to add  statins for the prevention of cardiovascular disease.

Patient underwent liver transplantation five months ago. Since most patients after solid organ transplantation receive CMV prophylaxis, ID physician was contacted to ensure whether the prescription was correct and not valgancyclovir should be given (which is indicated for CMV prophylaxis after solid organ transplantation). The order should be valganciclovir instead of valacyclovir.

records.error_type: Improper dose (over, under or extra dose)
records.error_cause: Patient knowledge Deficiency/Lab values /clinical information not available/not noted
records.Remarks/ Comments: Based on adequate renal function, clinical pharmacist suggested to make the dose 1g Q8h.

records.detected_by: Lab results were checked by Pharmacist
records.Remarks/ Comments: According to renal function impairment (GFR <25 mL/min), pharmacist has suggested to make the dose 0.5g IV Q12h.

records.detected_by: Lab results were checked by Pharmacist
records.Remarks/ Comments: According to renal function impairment (GFR <25 mL/min), pharmacist has suggested to make the dose 2.5M IV Q12h.
Omeprazol IV was given as Sress Ulcer Prophylaxis , which means that the dose should be 40 mg OD

records.error_type: Improper dose (over, under or extra dose)
records.Remarks/ Comments: The pediatric dose of cefotaxime should be 50 mg/kg/dose Q8h. The physician was contacted to make the dose 300 mg IV Q8h.

records.error_type: Improper dose (over, under or extra dose)
records.Remarks/ Comments: According to the guideline apixaban should be given twice daily. Resident physician was contacted to change the dose to 2.5 mg Q12h.

records.ACTION TAKEN: Send pharmacists note to physician for clarification
records.Remarks/ Comments: The dose of 300 mg ocrelizumab should be given approximately over 2.5 hours. The prescriber was contacted to confirm that the order should be 300 mg IV over 2.5 hours instead of 25 hours.

records.error_type: Improper dose (over, under or extra dose)
records.Remarks/ Comments: The (pediatric) loading dose of teicoplanin should not exceed 10 mg/kg. The given dose was about 18 mg/kg (230 mg / 13 kg). Pharmacist suggested to make the dose 130 mg instead of 230 mg.

records.error_type: Improper dose (over, under or extra dose)
records.Remarks/ Comments: According to patient’s bodyweight (80 kg) and patients age (80 years old), 2 gram of vancomycin (15 mg/kg) was considered as a supratherapeutic dose. Pharmacist suggested to make the dose 1 g IV once.

records.detected_by: Lab results were checked by Pharmacist
records.Remarks/ Comments: According to patients' renal function (GFR = 13 mL/min), adenuric dose should be adjusted to 40 mg PO OD. The nephrologist was contacted to change the dose.

records.detected_by: Lab results were checked by Pharmacist
records.Remarks/ Comments: According to patients' renal function (GFR>10 mL/min) Augmentin dose should be given twice daily. ID physician was contacted to change the dose.

records.error_type: Improper dose (over, under or extra dose)
records.detected_by: Lab results were checked by Pharmacist
records.Remarks/ Comments: Since the patient has an adequate renal function (GFR> 30 mL/min), CP has suggested to give a full dose of ertapenem (1 g IV Q24h).

records.error_cause: Medication knowledge Deficiency/Indications for medication use
records.Remarks/ Comments: Both formulations contain olanzapine as active ingredient. Suggested to discontinue one of both medications.

records.error_type: Omission error(No drug for medical condition)
records.Remarks/ Comments: Meropenem was started for the treatment of a wound infection. The infection was caused by Klebsiella pneumonia-ESBL and multidrug resistant Pseudomona aeruginosa, which is not susceptible to meropenem. ID physician was contacted to suggest addition of colistin on patients' antimicrobial treatment.
Neostigmine was given post OR. No order was given to discontinue.

records.error_type: Improper dose (over, under or extra dose)
records.Remarks/ Comments: Suggested to discontinue

According to the guidelines cellcept should be given in a dose of 1g twice daily. Since the patient has an infection, attending physician decided to decrease the dose of this immunosuppressant to 1g OD. Since twice daily administration of cellcept helps keep drug levels steady in the bloodstream, improving effectiveness and minimizing side effects, attending physician was advised to make the dose 500mg PO Q12h.
